actionable_feedback: Provide a parser-compatible spreadsheet (so openpyxl or equivalent can parse it) OR include a plain-text export of key formula cells. At minimum include the exact formulas used for normalization, weighting, sensitivity shift, ranking, and final classification (cell addresses + formulas).
Add a short cost breakdown (line-items) to support the asserted 'within 70,000 USD envelope' decision. Place this in the memo and optionally as a worksheet/table in the spreadsheet.

Document the explicit packet fields or exact cell/formula checks that triggered the override for R1. Provide a two-line justification in the memo that references cell addresses or packet field names and values so reviewers can confirm the logic without opening the workbook.
If possible, re-generate the XLSX using a library/version compatible with graders (openpyxl) or include a CSV/CSV+formulas export showing computed columns, so automated preview/parsing will succeed.
Add a short 'Repro checklist' section in the memo listing the sheets, key formula cell ranges, and the expected outputs to speed verification.

feedback_summary: Submission meets the artifact contract and documents methodology, weights, and sensitivity checks. Major issues prevent full reproducibility verification: the XLSX could not be parsed in the grader/verifier environment (openpyxl unavailable) so cell-level formulas and in-file derivations could not be independently inspected; the memo asserts the recommended intervention fits within the 70,000 USD envelope but lacks a line-item cost breakdown; and the top-quartile override for R1 (from an expected 'immediate_action' to 'needs_review') needs a concise explicit justification tied to packet-only fields or cell references.

limitations: Could not parse spreadsheet binary to verify cell-level formulas (openpyxl unavailable).
Some assertions (intervention costs, explicit cell references) not visible in parsed preview and reduce confidence.

recommended_next_steps_quick: Attach a text table of key formulas (sheet, cell, formula).
Add budget line-items to the memo.
Add a two-line packet-field/cell justification for the R1 override.
